format_discipline.detail: No frontmatter — first non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and parses with word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Section template: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways present in order; References validly absent (zero citations — narrative_seed_only mode, resource_count 0). Sidecar sections list matches the file.

findings.detail: Sidecar declares 1592 words (in range); independent markdown-stripped count is ~1652, ~3% over the 1600 ceiling — within counting-method noise (table cells, caption, headings). Read-aloud minutes 1652/230 = 7.2, cleanly inside 6-8. Treated as a boundary warning, not a hard length fail; surfaces at HITL #3.

citations.detail: Zero primary resources exist (resource_count: 0); citations_used is empty; no inline [N] markers, no bibliography — consistent. No orphans either direction. No fabricated citations.

forward_references.detail: All reused terms trace to prior topics: deterministic (1.2), probabilistic (1.3), pseudocode (1.7, week 1 — verified in sequence.index.json), algorithm (1.9), autocomplete/spam/recommendations/fraud examples (1.10), specification/testable/bounded/observable (2.1), failure conditions (2.3). 'Machine learning' appears exactly once (line 53) as a bare name + Week 3 pointer — no bolding, no defining clause; mandated mention-and-defer form. Strict week-3 ML mechanism scan: zero occurrences of training/model/weights/features/classifier/dataset/neural network/deep learning/LLM. 'Learns/learned' (lines 76, 81) is colloquial everyday use mirroring the corpus, not technical vocabulary. The rule-from-repetition mechanism is taught generically and sits squarely inside the headline 'how machines find rules in repeated data'.

faithfulness.detail: Sampled across all sections: chai-stall pattern, winner 40%/1% statistic, three-step see/form/apply loop, generalisation + overgeneralisation definitions, multiple-rules-fit-2-4-6-8 argument, three failure modes, fill-gaps-with-average-pattern claim, 5-step held-back-case procedure, all four In Practice systems, the one-sentence carry-forward. All trace verbatim or as faithful paraphrase to the corpus. 'Returns later this week' is a safe generalisation of the corpus's 'returns in topic 2.8'. 0 unsupported (threshold >2).

beginner_readability.detail: All four new terms (pattern, pattern recognition, generalisation, overgeneralisation) bold-defined in plain language on first use; no unexpanded acronyms; no paragraph stacks 3+ new terms; concrete-first throughout (2-4-6-8 sequence opens the reading, chai stall precedes the abstraction's use, junk-pile walkthrough before the rule claim); sentences short, 0 hedging phrases. 0 distinct findings.

findings.detail: 'More than one rule can fit the same examples' is a single 6-sentence run-on paragraph (config target ~4). The corpus rendered the alternative candidate rules as bullets (corpus lines 113-117); the reading collapsed them into inline prose. Recommend restoring the bullet list for the two alternative rules and splitting the paragraph.

formatting.detail: Otherwise strong: procedures rendered as numbered lists (3-step loop, 5-step worked example), comparison rendered as a table, bold-term/plain-meaning definition shape used consistently, inline examples present at every concept introduction (not deferred to Worked Example). One soft finding above.

assessment_reference.detail: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights anywhere in the reading. The gold 2-1 example's flagged A1 tie-in defect was correctly NOT replicated.

diagram_embed.detail: artifacts/diagram.png exists; reading embeds it at line 41 (./diagram.png — correct relative path from artifacts/) with descriptive alt text and an italic caption, placed at the 'two ways a machine gets a rule' concept it illustrates. Sidecar embeds_diagram: true matches.

scope.detail: Reading stays on the headline. The only adjacent-advanced concept, machine learning, is named once in bare-name+pointer form (line 53) and never taught. No new material was introduced beyond the corpus while condensing. 0 scope_creep findings — no escalation.

escalation: Checks 5-6 produced 1 distinct finding (wall_of_text) — below the >=3 escalation threshold; 0 scope_creep — no hard-fail escalation.
gold_comparison: Meets the 2-1 gold bar for standard-depth conceptual topics: bold-term plain definitions on first use, prior-concept recap before building on it, concrete-before-abstract per section, comparison table, paragraphs predominantly <=4 sentences, and — per the gold notes' explicit warning — no assessment tie-in. Wider than the gold's ~1200 words (~1650) but inside the standard profile's allowance; not 'markedly wider'.
verdict: PASS — clean, learner-facing reading faithful to the approved corpus. Format discipline holds (no frontmatter, valid sidecar, exact five-section template with References validly absent at zero citations). Forward-reference and strict week-3 ML scans are clean: 'machine learning' appears exactly once as a bare name + Week 3 pointer (line 53), zero training/model/neural-net vocabulary, mechanism taught generically inside the headline. Faithfulness 10/10 sampled assertions supported. Diagram correctly embedded (line 41) with alt text and caption. No assessment references — the gold example's flagged A1 defect was not copied. Two soft findings roll up to HITL #3: wall_of_text at line 69 (6-sentence paragraph; corpus's bullet list of alternative rules collapsed to prose) and word_count_upper_bound (meta 1592 in range vs ~1652 measured, ~3% over ceiling; minutes 7.2 in range). Worst finding: wall_of_text line 69.
